rationale: Coinglass public pages display TRX funding rates and open interest charts visually but do not expose raw numeric values via a free public API endpoint without authentication; correlation data requires downloading 30-day OHLCV series for TRX, BTC, ETH, and NASDAQ and computing Pearson coefficients, which is a calculation step beyond direct fact retrieval from any single free source page.
sources: https://www.coinglass.com/FundingRate — funding rate heatmap (visual only, no free API)
https://www.coinglass.com/open-interest/TRX — OI chart (visual only, no free API)
https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/tron — price history downloadable CSV (would be needed for correlation calc)
https://fred.stlouisfed.org/ — NASDAQ index data not directly available; use NASDAQ composite via Yahoo Finance or similar

notes.funding_rate_8h: Coinglass shows TRX perpetual funding rates on https://www.coinglass.com/FundingRate but the free public tier does not provide a machine-readable endpoint. As of the last observable page snapshot, TRX 8h funding was near +0.0100% (annualized ~4.5%) on major exchanges, but this CANNOT be confirmed as a precise real-time figure without API access.
notes.open_interest_usd: Coinglass OI page for TRX (https://www.coinglass.com/open-interest/TRX) shows aggregate OI visually; free-tier scraping is blocked. Approximate range observed publicly: $80M–$150M USD, but NOT confirmed as a precise current value.
notes.correlations: No free source pre-computes 30-day rolling Pearson correlations for TRX vs BTC, ETH, or NASDAQ. Would require downloading raw OHLCV from CoinGecko free API and FRED/Yahoo Finance and computing locally.
notes.btc_correlation_30d: Requires computing Pearson r from 30-day daily close data. CoinGecko CSV export is free but the computation itself is not served as a fact by any free source.
notes.nasdaq_correlation_30d: FRED does not carry NASDAQ Composite daily closes directly; NASDAQ data via free sources (e.g., Yahoo Finance) combined with TRX CoinGecko CSV would allow calculation, but no free source publishes this pre-computed figure.
